MIME-Version: 1.0
From: Every <hello@every.to>
To: '' <emil@skogsund.se>
Date: Wed, 25 Mar 2026 15:41:32 +0000
Subject: Instagram's Cofounder on Why Great Products Are Still Hard to Build
Message-ID: <20260325154132.86979864d2895071@mg.every.to>
Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8"

<!DOCTYPE html>

    <!-- The title is useful for screenreaders reading a document. Use your sender name or subject line. -->
    <title>Instagram's Cofounder on Why Great Products Are Still Hard to Build</title>
    <meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8">

.4em 0px;font-weight:700;line-height:1.25em}.article h2{font-size:30px}.article h3{font-size:28px}.article h4{font-size:24px}.article h5{font-size:20px}.article h6{font-size:18px}.article span{position:absolute;margin-top:-32px;margin-left:9px;height:22px;padding:5px;background:rgba(55,55,55,.9);color:#fff;border-radius:3px;font-size:12px;font-weight:bold;line-height:12px}.quill-tweet .quoted-tweet .quote-tweet-name{font-weight:bold;color:#1c2022;padding-right:5px}.quill-tweet a .quoted-tweet .quote-tweet-username{font-size:14px;color:#697882}.quill-tweet a .tweet-footer{margin-top:10px;font-size:14px;color:#697882}.quill-tweet a .tweet-footer .tweet-date{padding-bottom:10px;margin-bottom:0px !important;font-size:14px;color:#697882}.quill-tweet a .tweet-footer .interaction-wrapper .interaction .count{font-weight:bold;color:#1c2022;text-transform:uppercase}.quill-youtube a{display:block;position:relative}.quill-youtube a     <!-- Some preview text. -->
      <div class="preview-text" style="display: none; font-size: 1px; color: #333333; line-height: 1px; max-height: 0px; max-width: 0px; opacity: 0; overflow: hidden;">
          Plus, how important are technical skills in the age of vibe coding?
         ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
      </div>

    <table cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" border="0" width="100%" align="center" role="presentation"><tr><td>
      <div class="article" role="article" aria-label="Instagram's Cofounder on Why Great Products Are Still Hard to Build" lang="en">

          <!-- Main content section. Main is a useful landmark element. -->
          <main>
            <a class="logo" href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==">
              <img class="logo-img" width="84" height="24" src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/static/every-logo.png">
            </a>
            
<div class="newsletter-email">
  <div class="post-intro">
      <div class="publication-brand">
        <a class="publication-brand-link" href="https://every.">
            <img class="publication-logo" width="50" height="50" src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/publication/logo/94/small_context_windown_1.png">
            <p class="publication-name">Context Window</p>
        </a>
      </div>
    <h1 class="title">
        <a href="https://every.">Instagram’s Cofounder on Why Great <span class="hand hand-P">P</span>roducts Are Still Hard to Build</a>
    </h1>
      <h2 class="subtitle">
          <a href="https://every.">Plus, how important are technical skills in the age of vibe coding?</a>
      </h2>
    <div class="post-meta">
        <p class="byline">by <a href="https://every.">Laura Entis</a></p>
    </div>
      <figure>
        <a href="https://every." target="_blank" rel="noopener" style="text-decoration:none;">
          <span style="display:inline-block; position:relative;">
            <img class="post-cover" src="https://img.youtube.com/vi/KRv9GpJYrUA/hqdefault.jpg" width="600" alt="Watch on YouTube">
            <span style="position:absolute; top:50%; left:50%; transform:translate(-50%, -50%);">
              <img src="https://every.to/play-solid-full.png" alt="Play" width="48" height="48" style="display:block; border:0;">
            </span>
            <span style="position:absolute; bottom:18px; right:18px; display:inline-block; padding:6px 10px; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.55); border-radius:22px;">
              <img src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/static/emails/youtube-logo.png" alt="YouTube" width="88" height="24" style="display:block; border:0;">
            </span>
          </span>
        </a>
          <figcaption>Mike Krieger. Midjourney/Every illustration.</figcaption>
      </figure>
  </div>

  <div class="post-body">
    <p><em>We’re excited to welcome </em><strong><em><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Laura Entis</a></u></em></strong><em> to Every as a staff writer. A former editor at Fortune and news editor at LinkedIn, Laura will cover Every as a beat—documenting how an AI-native media and software company works, our experiments, and our lessons. She writes today’s Context Window.—<u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Eleanor Warnock</a></u></em></p>
<p><em>Was this newsletter forwarded to you?<a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"> </a><u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Sign up</a></u> to get it in your inbox.</em></p>
<p></p>
<hr class="quill-line">
<p></p>
<h2>‘AI &amp; I’: How to build an agent-native product</h2>
<p>Today, we’re releasing a new episode of our podcast <em><u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3BvZGNhc3QiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">AI &amp; I</a></u></em>. <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Dan Shipper</a></u></strong> sits down with <strong>Mike Krieger</strong>, cofounder of Instagram and co-lead of Anthropic Labs—the team working on experimental projects within the Claude developer—to discuss how the rules of professional product development are being rewritten in real time. Krieger brings a rare perspective as someone who has been at the frontier of two transformative technology waves, the mobile and social boom, and now agent-native software.</p>
<p>Watch on <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">X</a> or <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">YouTube</a>, or listen on <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Spotify</a> or <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Apple Podcasts</a>. You can also read <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">the transcript</a>.</p>
<p>Here are the highlights:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>A truly agent-native product can flex to meet user demand.</strong> The best products today, like Claude Code, allow users to do things that their creators never intended. But that requires hard trade-offs between freedom and reliability for frontier products, an issue that Krieger’s team is learning how to solve. </li>
<li>
<strong>Timelines are the difference between building now and building at Instagram.</strong> At Instagram, it took months to hit dead ends and learn what to cut, Krieger says. Now, that cycle runs in hours.</li>
<li>
<strong>Building too much, too fast with agents is a trap.</strong> You can go from idea to a nearly-shipped product in a day, but that process doesn’t give you the incremental feedback that used to tell you what not to build. The models are great at adding features, but can create a product that lacks coherence.</li>
<li>
<strong>Anthropic Labs structures product teams to be lean.</strong> New product experiments are led by only two people, usually a product manager or designer paired with an engineer. Krieger says bigger teams tend to be too slow because of coordination complications.</li>
<li>
<strong>You need to throw out your product and start over every three to six months.</strong> AI progress means most of your software infrastructure will be outdated quickly. The best teams build this into their product strategy.</li>
</ul>
<p>Miss an episode? Catch up on Dan’s recent conversations with LinkedIn cofounder <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Reid Hoffman</a></u></strong>; the team that built Claude Code, <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Cat Wu</a> </u></strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">and</a> </u><strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Boris Cherny</a></u></strong>; Vercel cofounder <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Guillermo Rauch</a></u></strong>; podcaster <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Dwarkesh Patel</a></u></strong>; and others, and learn how they use AI to think, create, and relate.</p>  <div class="ea-block normal">
      <h4 class="ea-block-label"></h4>
      <div class="ea-block-inner">
<div class="quill-block-image" id="quill-block-image-1774450308241" data-source='{"dom_id":"quill-block-image-1774450308241","link":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/advertisements/1047/optimized_32f05ea3-17dc-4d01-aeff-38b32151ec5c.png","image":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/advertisements/1047/optimized_32f05ea3-17dc-4d01-aeff-38b32151ec5c.png","caption":null,"error":null}'><div><a href="https://every." target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" data-advertisement-id="1047"><img src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/advertisements/1047/optimized_32f05ea3-17dc-4d01-aeff-38b32151ec5c.png" alt="Uploaded image" width="600"></a></div></div>
<p><br></p>
<p><strong>Don’t let infrastructure slow down your AI app</strong></p>
<p>Your fine-tuned model is ready, but your infrastructure isn’t. Sound familiar? Crusoe Managed Inference handles deployment and optimization so you can move from prototype to production with confidence. Their team helps you deploy, then get best-in-class performance. <strong>Now, as a Day One launch partner for NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super and Nemotron 3 VoiceChat, Crusoe gives you access to elite models, or bring your own, with 1M-token context windows.</strong> Get reliable performance that stays high quality even with long prompts.</p>
<div class="quill-button" data-source='{"id":"quill-button-1774450340131","text":"Learn more","url":"https://www.crusoe.ai/cloud/managed-inference?utm_source=smoothmedia&amp;utm_medium=newsletter&amp;utm_campaign=every&amp;source=post_button"}' id="quill-button-1774450340131"><a href="https://every._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1zbW9vdGhtZWRpYVx1MDAyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW09bmV3c2xldHRlclx1MDAyNnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1ldmVyeVx1MDAyNnNvdXJjZT1wb3N0X2J1dHRvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoyNCwiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTA0N30=" data-advertisement-id="1047">Learn more</a></div>
</div>
      <div class="ea-block-footer">
        <a href="https://every.">Want to sponsor Every? Click here</a><span>.</span>
</div>
  </div>
<h2>Does persistence beat technical skills? </h2>
<p>How important are technical skills in the age of vibe coding? We’ve been having this discussion at Every, and to some of us, the ability to build in plain English feels like the revenge of the liberal arts major. </p>
<p>But other skills are also needed. AI tools are now powerful enough that “the question of whether you can ship a product isn’t if you are technical or not—it’s whether you have the persistence,” says <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Mike Taylor</a></u></strong>, head of tech consulting at Every. </p>
<p>This persistence often takes the form of asking the AI to explain itself again and again and again. “The core skill is to ask for help,” says <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Natalia Quintero</a></u></strong>, Every’s head of consulting. “If you keep asking, you can become technical.” </p>
<p><strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Austin Tedesco</a></u></strong>, Every’s head of growth and a self-described “tech doofus,” built <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">an agent using Claude Code</a> that the entire team now uses to pull and analyze data about the company’s performance. Echoing Natalia’s comments, he says the key was having the doggedness to understand what was happening, the humility to ask Claude to explain it to him like he was an idiot whenever he didn’t follow, and the patience to refine the instructions when it didn’t give the right output or misinterpreted a command. </p>
<p>Persistence, clarity of vision, and curiosity can get you to a powerful prototype. But turning a prototype <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">into a reliable product</a></u>? For that, you need technical skills, or at least access to someone who has them, argues <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Naveen Naidu</a></u></strong>, general manager of Every’s dictation app <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Monologue</a></u></strong>.</p>
<p>An engineer, Naveen, never loses sight of a codebase’s overarching structure—something language models struggle to do. When Naveen builds a product, every decision ties back to one question: What can’t be allowed to fail? With Monologue, he knew that the vast majority of people would be using the app for dictation. If that feature failed, he’d immediately lose users’ trust. “I built my whole codebase in such a way that if my main server or database goes down, dictation doesn’t go down,” he says. “I made sure that it’s 100 percent reliable.”</p>
<p>Recently, the Monologue server did, indeed, go down. While new users couldn’t sign up, existing users could still dictate and didn’t notice any break in service. </p>
<p>Vibe coded apps often skip the information architecture step entirely. But when the code inevitably breaks, the non-technical user struggles to diagnose the problem, let alone fix it quickly. Having been the “technical” guy called upon to fix a vibe coded codebase, Naveen has found that rather than try to remedy existing structural issues, it’s sometimes easier to just start from scratch.—<u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Laura Entis</a></u> </p>
<h2>Log on</h2>
<p>We host <u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2V2ZW50cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjozNH0=" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">camps and workshops</a></u> on topics like <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">compound engineering</a></u> and <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">writing with AI</a></u> to share the knowledge we’ve acquired from training teams at companies like the<u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"> </a></u><em><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">New York Times</a></u></em><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"> and leading hedge funds</a></u>, and by learning and playing with AI every day ourselves.</p>
<h4><strong>This week’s camp</strong></h4>
<ul><li>Get a front-row seat to what we’re building at Every’s <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Q2 Demo Day</a></u></strong>, including a live walk-through of Plus One, our hosted AI agent that lives in Slack. The event takes place on Friday, March 27, at 11 a.m. ET. </li></ul>
<h4><strong>Upcoming courses</strong></h4>
<ul>
<li>
<strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Every x Notion: Custom Agents Camp</a></u> </strong>(April 3): Learn how to put custom agents to work inside your business with product designer <strong>Brian Lovin</strong> from Notion.</li>
<li>
<strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Claude Code for Absolute Beginners</a></u> </strong>(April 14): This beginner-friendly, live workshop led by <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Mike</a></u><strong>,</strong> Every’s head of tech consulting, is designed to get you from zero to a working project with Claude Code.</li>
</ul>
<h4><strong>Recordings you may have missed</strong></h4>
<ul>
<li>
<strong>Compound Engineering Camp: <u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2NvcmEuY29tcHV0ZXIvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjQ0fQ==" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Cora</a></u></strong> general manager<a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"> </a><strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Kieran Klaassen</a></u></strong> walks through, step by step, how to go from prompt to working app in under an hour using the <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">compound engineering</a></u> plugin. <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Watch the recording</a></u> or <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">read the write-up</a></u>.</li>
<li>
<strong>OpenClaw Camp:</strong> The Every team walks through <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">OpenClaw</a></u>, showing how to set it up  and our favorite use cases. <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Watch the recording</a></u> or <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">read the write-up</a></u>.</li>
</ul>
<h2>Straight from Slack </h2>
<div class="quill-block-image" id="quill-block-image-1774444976669-7wkttyuob" data-source='{"dom_id":"quill-block-image-1774444976669-7wkttyuob","link":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_850f08c1-6928-4bcf-a272-15642e96d89f.png","image":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_850f08c1-6928-4bcf-a272-15642e96d89f.png","caption":"A dashboard view of recent traces, showing a history of prompts and their responses across a session. (Screenshot courtesy of Mike Taylor.)","error":null}'><div>
<a href="https://every." target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><img src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_850f08c1-6928-4bcf-a272-15642e96d89f.png" alt="A dashboard view of recent traces, showing a history of prompts and their responses across a session. (Screenshot courtesy of Mike Taylor.)" width="600"></a><figcaption class="quill-image-caption">A dashboard view of recent traces, showing a history of prompts and their responses across a session. (Screenshot courtesy of Mike Taylor.)</figcaption>
</div></div>
<p><br></p>
<p>Prompts still form the backbone of AI workflows, but knowing whether your latest version is actually an improvement can be annoyingly difficult to track.</p>
<p>Our resident prompt expert, Mike, has a new favorite method to test and improve his prompts: <u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2xhbmdmdXNlLmNvbS8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NTR9" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Langfuse</a></u>, an open-source tool for tracking AI model behavior that he calls “Google analytics for AI.”  </p>
<p>Recently, when running a PowerPoint task, Mike noticed his agent was loading unnecessary skills, which cost him precious tokens. By using Langfuse to trace and label his Claude sessions, he was able to see, play by play, where the model made the wrong or right decision. “Otherwise, you have no idea if the changes you are making are helping or harming,” he says.</p>
<h2>One more thing</h2>
<p>How many engineers do you need to ship a breakout product? Just two—provided they fill specific roles, argues <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Dan</a></u>. </p>
<div class="quill-block-image" id="quill-block-image-1774444976674-4tzl0g88t" data-source='{"dom_id":"quill-block-image-1774444976674-4tzl0g88t","link":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_098b45c9-e68b-4663-81c1-91f314c791ce.png","image":"https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_098b45c9-e68b-4663-81c1-91f314c791ce.png","caption":"Dan’s X post about engineering teams in 2026. (Screenshot courtesy of Every.)","error":null}'><div>
<a href="https://every." target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><img src="https://d24ovhgu8s7341.cloudfront.net/uploads/editor/posts/4051/optimized_098b45c9-e68b-4663-81c1-91f314c791ce.png" alt="Dan’s X post about engineering teams in 2026. (Screenshot courtesy of Every.)" width="600"></a><figcaption class="quill-image-caption">Dan’s X post about engineering teams in 2026. (Screenshot courtesy of Every.)</figcaption>
</div></div>
<p><br></p>
<p>First, you need a pirate, or someone who has ambitious product ideas and moves as fast as possible to vibe code them into existence. </p>
<p>Once the pirate has established early product-market fit, the architect arrives to turn the prototype into something <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">that can reliably work at scale</a></u>.</p>
<p>We’ve already been using the pirate-architect model here at Every: <strong><u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Proof</a></u></strong>, our new online editor built for agents and humans to collaborate, was built that way—as was <u><a href="https://every._cz0yMCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1OX0=" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Monologue</a></u>.</p>
<p></p>
<hr class="quill-line">
<p></p>
<p><strong><em><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Laura Entis</a></em></strong><em> is a staff writer at Every. You can follow her on <a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">LinkedIn</a>. To read more, subscribe to <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Every</a></u>, and follow us on X at <u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwOi8vdHdpdHRlci5jb20vZXZlcnkiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NjN9" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">@every</a></u> and on <u><a href="https://every." rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">LinkedIn</a></u>.</em></p>
<p><em>We also do AI training, adoption, and innovation for companies. <u><a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnN1bHRpbmc_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1lbWFpbGZvb3RlciIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2NX0=" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Work with us</a></u> to bring AI into your organization. For sponsorship opportunities, reach out to sponsorships@every.to.</em></p>
<div class="quill-button" data-source='{"id":"quill-button-1769187301610","url":"https://every.to/subscribe?source=post_button","text":"Subscribe"}' id="quill-button-1769187301610"><a href="https://every.">Subscribe</a></div>

            </a>

            </a>

  </div>

  </div>

          </div>

          </div>

          </div>

      <!-- Bundle of AI software section -->
      <div style="display: table; width: 100%;">
        <div style="display: table-cell; width: 33%; vertical-align: top; padding-right: 8px;">
          <div style="display: flex; align-items: start; margin-bottom: 4px;">
            <img src="https://every.to/paywall/sparks.png" alt="Sparks" style="width: 14px; height: 14px; margin-right: 4px;">

          </div>
        </div>
        
        <div style="display: table-cell; width: 67%; vertical-align: top;">
          <div style="margin-bottom: 6px; line-height: 1;">
            <img src="https://every.to/paywall/app_icons/sparkle.png" alt="Sparkle" style="width: 14px; height: 14px; border-radius: 2px; margin-right: 2px; vertical-align: middle;">

          </div>

</div> 

  
</div>

          </main>

          <!-- Footer information. Footer is a useful landmark element. -->
          <footer>
              <!-- This link uses descriptive text to inform the user what will happen with the link is tapped. -->
              <!-- It also uses inline styles since some email clients won't render embedded styles from the head. -->
              <p>
                  You received this email because you signed up for emails from <a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjcyfQ==">Every</a>. Need help? Visit <a href="https://every.to/emails/click/f91b1e00930c1c7802951fb76da18da9d1d86023a9ec7b653e47ca1b5d584dbf/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSW5zdGFncmFtJ3MgQ29mb3VuZGVyIG9uIFdoeSBHcmVhdCBQcm9kdWN0cyBBcmUgU3RpbGwgSGFyZCB0byBCdWlsZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQwNTEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2hlbHAuZXZlcnkudG8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NzN9">our help center</a>. No longer interested in receiving emails from us? Click here to <a href="https://every.">unsubscribe</a>.
              </p>

              <!-- The address element does exactly what it says. By default, it is block-level and italicizes text. You can override this behavior inline. -->
              <address>
                  221 Canal St 5th floor, New York, NY 10013
              </address>
          </footer>

      </div>
    </td></tr></table>
